of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more of this may be controversial so I'd certainly like to hear from other usegalaxy.* admins before this is merged. =)

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more This one is on how to set up a subdomain 

The ultimate goal is to create a learning pathway that helps people to discover the communities existing (the sig tuto), then if needed set up a subdomain, then how to create tools and finally how to add those tools to the subdomain (a 2nd tuto in a another PR)

This one is on how to set up a subdomain 

The ultimate goal is to create a learning pathway that helps people to discover the communities existing (the sig tuto), then if needed set up a subdomain, then how to create tools and finally how to add those tools to the subdomain (a 2nd tuto in a another PR)

This one is on how to set up a subdomain 

The ultimate goal is to create a learning pathway that helps people to discover the communities existing (the sig tuto), then if needed set up a subdomain, then how to create tools and finally how to add those tools to the subdomain (a 2nd tuto in a another PR)

the bug**
We got a support request from a user, that ran out of storage and can not delete 2 large datasets, because they are not shown in the history (independent of the filter combinations).
In the storage manager, the datasets are listed and you can also find them in `User→Datasets`, but when you then click on `view in history` the datasets will not appear (filters: `deleted:any visible:any hid:61`).
However, what appears is the compressed version, which has the same hid.
When I looked the datasets up in our database using 
~~~
gxadmin query q "select dataset_id, d.uuid, name, hid, visible, d.deleted, d.purged, d.file_size  \
from history_dataset_association inner join dataset d on dataset_id = d.id where history_id = xxxxx and d.purged = 'f'"
~~~
The datasets showed up as follows:
~~~
dataset_id |                                name                                | hid | visible | deleted | purged |  file_size        
------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+---------+---------+--------+--------------
...
  xxxxxxx55 | Concatenate datasets on data 53, data 57, and data 55 uncompressed |  61 | f       | f       | f      | 166396579020
  xxxxxxx58 | Concatenate datasets on data 63, data 65, and data 64 uncompressed |  66 | f       | f       | f      | 166396579020
~~~
**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.1_europe
Commit:  5af8ba0f4c2df9f1fdac11acc3f282023322690c

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Not sure how
The user reported that two jobs were running and only one finished and the other one was paused due to using ~200% of disk quota.
Galaxy shows the following message in the paused job:
~~~
Execution of this dataset's job is paused because you were over your disk quota at the time it was ready to run
~~~

**Expected behavior**
Dataset shown in the history and can be deleted and purged;
the compressed and the uncompressed version is visible and the uncompressed version can be deleted.
**Screenshots**

![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 09-06-26-censored](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/0360e156-01d2-4ad9-bba5-ab487d8d1094)
![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 09-05-27-censored](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/d4a55791-ec0f-4309-8b61-a4dd663fa195)
![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 16-18-21](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/a5b8887f-7864-44d5-a846-c377fb150357)

![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 16-18-39](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/51d47fb9-03b7-4a13-9c40-1065056be48c)

to https://download.postgresql.org/pub/repos/yum/keys/README.txt, there are two types of keys for RHEL: `RPM-GPG-KEY-PGDG*` and `PGDG-RPM-GPG-KEY*`.

> This directory contains keys used for signing the PostgreSQL RPMs on RHEL (and its derivatives) and Fedora.
> The keys which have the PGDG prefix are used to sign PostgreSQL 11 and above RPMs.
>
> Archived versions (v10 and below) use the RPM-GPG-KEY prefix.

The role is already adding the first kind of key to the system (see #53), but not the second. Therefore, installing newer PostgreSQL versions is not possible at the moment. This commit adds the second type of key so that newer PostgreSQL versions can be installed too.

Closes {"repository_id":30035910,"push_id":20111154832,"size":2,"distinct_size":2,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"52220795dd3aa04c026846442c08e0561f720760","before":"53bf3600a730002425a0e51cd31aa7c6f8cb35cc","commits":[{"sha":"d944281bf8a8dceb56830b549c343a31badcb818","author":{"email":"000679812392b5c9ca992824a15e84f088284afd@users.noreply.github.com","name":"José Manuel Domínguez"},"message":"Add RHEL GPG key with PGDG prefix (allows installing PostgreSQL >= 11)

According to https://download.postgresql.org/pub/repos/yum/keys/README.txt, there are two types of keys for RHEL: `RPM-GPG-KEY-PGDG*` and `PGDG-RPM-GPG-KEY*`.

> This directory contains keys used for signing the PostgreSQL RPMs on RHEL (and its derivatives) and Fedora.
> The keys which have the PGDG prefix are used to sign PostgreSQL 11 and above RPMs.
>
> Archived versions (v10 and below) use the RPM-GPG-KEY prefix.

The role is already adding the first kind of key to the system, but not the second. Therefore, installing newer PostgreSQL versions is not possible at the moment. This commit adds the second type of key so that newer PostgreSQL versions can be installed too.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-postgresql/commits/d944281bf8a8dceb56830b549c343a31badcb818"},{"sha":"52220795dd3aa04c026846442c08e0561f720760","author":{"email":"f2eacde93615a1042352f41c188ca4152a829c6e@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Mira"},"message":"Merge pull request #58 from kysrpex/gpg_keys

> This directory contains keys used for signing the PostgreSQL RPMs on RHEL (and its derivatives) and Fedora.
> The keys which have the PGDG prefix are used to sign PostgreSQL 11 and above RPMs.
>
> Archived versions (v10 and below) use the RPM-GPG-KEY prefix.

The role is already adding the first kind of key to the system (see #53), but not the second. Therefore, installing newer PostgreSQL versions is not possible at the moment. This commit adds the second type of key so that newer PostgreSQL versions can be installed too.

Closes function","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-12T14:13:42Z","updated_at":"2024-09-12T14:13:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We discussed about how to inform users that their ITs will be terminated after 24h. There were several suggestions: The website, training material, as static text in the 'running ITs' window and a notification that is send to users.
So I came to the idea that maybe TPV can send notifications to the user via the Galaxy API. These could be specified e.g. similar to the `fail` message and be triggered by a rule like:
~~~
- if: input_size > 10
  notify:
    msg: 
    subject: "Tool suggestion for *small file Tool*",
    message: "Please consider using the *tool for larger input files*",
    category: "info"
# OR
- if: true
  notify:
    subject: "24h lifetime for Interactive Tools",
    message: "For giving everyone a shorter waiting time we terminate Interactive tools after 24h",
    category: "warning"

~~~

Please share your opinions and ideas","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/139/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/139/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

usegalaxy.*/api/docs
is not loading for me:
![image](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/e70818a7-feba-4c37-a39a-064ca92ceadf)


The issue is an invalid certificate for a Cloudflare CDN server, it seems to be expired in 2020.
After googleing it, I found this:
https://community.cloudflare.com/t/jsdelivr-expired-certificate-may-2024/650543

It could be a bigger issue.
Sure we can not do anything about it and CDNs *should* always work, but in this case I was wondering if it is really needed
or if we could serve this with our own servers?

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 24.0
all 3 usegalaxy.* servers

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Linux, macOS
Browser: Firefox,  Chrome-based, Safari

Firefox 125.2 (fedora linux 39)
Chromium 124.0.6367.91 (fedora linux 39)
Safari on iPadOS 17.4.1

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Maybe it is location dependent, but otherwise the steps are quite obivous

**Expected behavior**
swagger api docs should load

After googleing it, I found this:
https://community.cloudflare.com/t/jsdelivr-expired-certificate-may-2024/650543
and
https://github.com/jsdelivr/jsdelivr/issues/18565

It could be a bigger issue.
Sure we can not do anything about it and CDNs *should* always work, but in this case I was wondering if it is really needed
or if we could serve this with our own servers?

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 24.0
all 3 usegalaxy.* servers

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Linux, macOS
Browser: Firefox,  Chrome-based, Safari

Firefox 125.2 (fedora linux 39)
Chromium 124.0.6367.91 (fedora linux 39)
Safari on iPadOS 17.4.1

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Maybe it is location dependent, but otherwise the steps are quite obivous

**Expected behavior**
swagger api docs should load

we upgraded EU to 24.0, we noticed, that some subdomains were having old CSS files.
see (not public):
- https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/issues/issues/537

This comes potentially from the `ignore_existing` flag I set to rsync the files from Galaxy's default static directory to the subdomain's static directory. The reason was, that ansible would touch files that are different for the subdomain on every plabook run. It would first overwrite them with the rsync command and than overwrite them again when it copies over subdomain's custom file.
My idea to solve this would be using this:
`{{ __galaxy_client_build_version }} != {{ __galaxy_current_commit_id }}`
as indicator that Galaxy has been updated and in that case omit the `ignore_existing` flag. Does that sound sensible to you
@hexylena @natefoo or do you think there is a better approach?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/issues/213/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/issues/213/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

`INSTALL_PREBUILT_CLIENT=1` in front of `run.sh` will use a prebuilt client from npm

Perhaps we can try switching to the prebuilt client in admin training, it'd get some more exposure/testing of the feature which could be quite good, and would let us switch from 8GB VMs back to the 2GB VMs so we can have more trainees supported in each event which benefits everyone. I think the node step is also probably ~80% of our wait time during the installation step so this would be fantastic for us.

xref https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14948

cc @mira-miracoli @cat-bro @natefoo >gxadmin gunicorn handler-restart
currently restarts one gunicorn instance after another, which leads to very long restart times (and therefore very long playbook runs on the headnodes)

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17503","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17503/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17503/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17503/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17503","id":2144027423,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5_y0Mf","number":17503,"title":"Encode ID API endpoint missing","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-02-20T10:17:45Z","updated_at":"2024-02-20T10:17:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Not really a bug, but a feature that is missing.
If I want to interact with the galaxy API and for example delete users, like the [WALL·E script](https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/WallE) is doing, you need to be able to encode galaxy IDs.
You could do this with the `secret_decoder_ring.py`, but you would need to open subprocess and source Galaxy's virtualenv and pass Galaxy's config file with the id_secret.
This seems to be a bit hacky to me and there might be scenarios where WALL·E runs on nodes, that can not access this secret and/or Galaxy's virtualenv. (Even though I would need to change a few things to reduce dependencis for sure.)

I understand that this is not an important feature to implement, but maybe it could serve as a good first issue.

**Expected behavior**
An API endpoint like
`/api/configuration/encode/{decoded_id}`

{"action":"opened","number":17246,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/17246","id":1666472325,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc5jVFmF","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17246","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17246.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17246.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17246","number":17246,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"tus wants a json response from v2.0.0","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"
My tus was not working, with version `v2.2.1`, and Galaxy release `23.1`, giving me this error:
`level=INFO event=ResponseOutgoing method=POST path="" requestId="" status=500 body="ERR_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR: failed to parse hook response: unexpected end of JSON input\n" `
When I changed [this line](https://github.com/tus/tusd/blob/b7b875a7d6864b050351325d90bb35b3ae73556e/pkg/hooks/http/http.go#L80) to print the Response body, I got '[]'. So I checked Galaxy's source code and indded, it seems to respond with a raw body, which throws an error in Go's JSON.Unmarshall [example](https://play.golang.com/p/rrNfRYdxsCz)
https://github.com/tus/tusd/pull/516
Was the breaking change I guess. I tried with tus `v1.13.0` and that still worked for me. Could it be possible that it already broke down after they released `v2.0.0` on September 29 2023?

more info in tus' docs: 
https://github.com/tus/tusd/blob/main/docs/hooks.md#hook-requests-and-responses

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17220","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17220/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17220/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17220/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17220","id":2050769680,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc56PEMQ","number":17220,"title":"Dataset not visible in a user's history (compressed/uncompressed, same hid)","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-12-20T15:21:59Z","updated_at":"2023-12-20T15:21:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
We got a support request from a user, that ran out of storage and can not delete 2 large datasets, because they are not shown in the history (independent of the filter combinations).
In the storage manager, the datasets are listed and you can also find them in `User→Datasets`, but when you then click on `view in history` the datasets will not appear (filters: `deleted:any visible:any hid:61`).
However, what appears is the compressed version, which has the same hid.
When I looked the datasets up in our database using 
~~~
gxadmin query q "select dataset_id, d.uuid, name, hid, visible, d.deleted, d.purged, d.file_size  \
from history_dataset_association inner join dataset d on dataset_id = d.id where history_id = xxxxx and d.purged = 'f'"
~~~
The datasets showed up as follows:
~~~
dataset_id |                                name                                | hid | visible | deleted | purged |  file_size        
------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------+-----+---------+---------+--------+--------------
...
  xxxxxxx55 | Concatenate datasets on data 53, data 57, and data 55 uncompressed |  61 | f       | f       | f      | 166396579020
  xxxxxxx58 | Concatenate datasets on data 63, data 65, and data 64 uncompressed |  66 | f       | f       | f      | 166396579020
~~~
**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.1_europe
Commit:  5af8ba0f4c2df9f1fdac11acc3f282023322690c

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Not sure how
The user reported that two jobs were running and only one finished and the other one was paused due to using ~200% of disk quota.
Galaxy shows the following message in the paused job:
~~~
Execution of this dataset's job is paused because you were over your disk quota at the time it was ready to run
~~~

**Expected behavior**
Dataset shown in the history and can be deleted and purged;
the compressed and the uncompressed version is visible and the uncompressed version can be deleted.
**Screenshots**

![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 09-06-26-censored](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/0360e156-01d2-4ad9-bba5-ab487d8d1094)
![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 09-05-27-censored](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/d4a55791-ec0f-4309-8b61-a4dd663fa195)
![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 16-18-21](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/a5b8887f-7864-44d5-a846-c377fb150357)

![Screenshot from 2023-12-20 16-18-39](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/86979912/51d47fb9-03b7-4a13-9c40-1065056be48c)

encountered a few problems yesterday, when I helped [@engynasr](https://github.com/engynasr) to upload her datasets in a Data Library.

1.  The folder upload is only possible for admin users, and only by rsync-ing the files to our galaxy's server
2.  After the upload, I was able to import the files with the admin-only path-import-tool, but it showed an red error pop-up and I dont know if everything worked correctly.
3.  The permissions are automatically set to my user only for all the datasets and I don't see a way to change this efficiently (we have 942 Files, every single one would have to be changed manually

My suggestions:
 - implement a folder upload for users in the Data Libraries. This could also be tied to a certain role or manual permission from admins to prevent abuse
 - make it possible to mutate permissions for all datasets in folders and sub-folders at once
 - fix popups or show errors more accurately and remove '200% completed' text in upload window.
 - if we keep the admin path upload, please specify which paths are allowed, because not all locations worked for me
 
Thank you in advance. I think this could make life easier for both admins and users :)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

100% sure where to place this issue, but I thought it might be interesting for all users of the shared database. Otherwise I can of course move it to EU.

I am currently debugging an error the pepquery2 tool. The job errored because the JVM run out of memory.
When I tried to run the job locally I had to stop at 14G because my laptop (16G) started to lag.
I noticed that:
- it uses 8 cores (from the logs) even though it was allocated 1 core
- the index creation set is much slower on the server than on my laptop (might be storage related)
- there is no rule for TPV (and was no rule for sortinghat)

I would like to change that, but I am not sure which values I should consider. In their documentation I found a recommendation for `8 GB of memory and 4 CPUs` which is too little for at least the job I am looking at. When I tried to use `gxadmin query tool-memory-per-inputs` I found:
```
    id    |                                tool_id                                 | input_count | total_input_size_mb | mean_input_size_mb | median_input_size_mb | memory_used_mb | memory_used_per_input_mb | memory_mean_input_ratio | memory_median_input_ratio                                                                                                               
----------+------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------+---------------------+--------------------+----------------------+----------------+--------------------------+-------------------------+--------------------------- 
 ######### | toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/galaxyp/pepquery2/pepquery2/2.0.2+galaxy0 |           1 |                  36 |                 36 |                   36 |         283829 |            7948 |                    7948 |                      7948                           
```

While `gxadmin report job-info` returned the following:

```
## Destination Parameters                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                 
Key | Value                                                                                                                                                                                  
--- | ---
+Group | `""`
accounting_group_user | `#####`
description | `pepquery2`                                                                                                                                                                    
docker_memory | `3.8G`                                                    
metadata_strategy | `extended`                                                               
request_cpus | `1`                                                                                                                                                                           
request_memory | `3.8G`
requirements | `(GalaxyGroup  ==  "compute")`
submit_request_gpus | `0`    
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/30/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tpv-shared-database/issues/30/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Otherwise I can of course move it to EU.

I am currently debugging an error the pepquery2 tool. The job errored because the JVM run out of memory.
When I tried to run the job locally I had to stop at 14G because my laptop (16G) started to lag.
I noticed that:
- it uses 8 cores (from the logs) even though it was allocated 1 core
- the index creation set is much slower on the server than on my laptop (might be storage related)
- there is no rule for TPV (and was no rule for sortinghat)

I would like to change that, but I am not sure which values I should consider. In their documentation I found a recommendation for `8 GB of memory and 4 CPUs` which is too little for at least the job I am looking at. When I tried to use `gxadmin query tool-memory-per-inputs` I found:
```
    id    |                                tool_id                                 | input_count | total_input_size_mb | mean_input_size_mb | median_input_size_mb | memory_used_mb | memory_used_per_input_mb | memory_mean_input_ratio | memory_median_input_ratio                                                                                                               
----------+------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------+---------------------+--------------------+----------------------+----------------+--------------------------+-------------------------+--------------------------- 
 ######### | toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/galaxyp/pepquery2/pepquery2/2.0.2+galaxy0 |           1 |                  36 |                 36 |                   36 |         283829 |            7948 |                    7948 |                      7948                           
```

While `gxadmin report job-info` returned the following:

```
## Destination Parameters                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                 
Key | Value                                                                                                                                                                                  
--- | ---
+Group | `""`
accounting_group_user | `#####`
description | `pepquery2`                                                                                                                                                                    
docker_memory | `3.8G`                                                    
metadata_strategy | `extended`                                                               
request_cpus | `1`                                                                                                                                                                           
request_memory | `3.8G`
requirements | `(GalaxyGroup  ==  "compute")`
submit_request_gpus | `0`    
```

I am now trying to figure out how to implement a rule here and if we have to change something in the wrapper because of the CPU usage. Otherwise I can of course move it to EU.

I am currently debugging an error the pepquery2 tool. The job errored because the JVM run out of memory.
When I tried to run the job locally I had to stop at 14G because my laptop (16G) started to lag.
I noticed that:
- it uses 8 cores (from the logs) even though it was allocated 1 core
- the index creation set is much slower on the server than on my laptop (might be storage related)
- there is no rule for TPV (and was no rule for sortinghat)

I would like to change that, but I am not sure which values I should consider. In their documentation I found a recommendation for `8 GB of memory and 4 CPUs` which is too little for at least the job I am looking at. When I tried to use `gxadmin query tool-memory-per-inputs` I found:
```
    id    |                                tool_id                                 | input_count | total_input_size_mb | mean_input_size_mb | median_input_size_mb | memory_used_mb | memory_used_per_input_mb | memory_mean_input_ratio | memory_median_input_ratio                                                                                                               
----------+------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------+---------------------+--------------------+----------------------+----------------+--------------------------+-------------------------+--------------------------- 
 ######### | toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/galaxyp/pepquery2/pepquery2/2.0.2+galaxy0 |           1 |                  36 |                 36 |                   36 |         283829 |            7948 |                    7948 |                      7948                           
```

While `gxadmin report job-info` returned the following:

```
## Destination Parameters                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                 
Key | Value                                                                                                                                                                                  
--- | ---
+Group | `""`
accounting_group_user | `#####`
description | `pepquery2`                                                                                                                                                                    
docker_memory | `3.8G`                                                    
metadata_strategy | `extended`                                                               
request_cpus | `1`                                                                                                                                                                           
request_memory | `3.8G`
requirements | `(GalaxyGroup  ==  "compute")`
submit_request_gpus | `0`    
```

100% sure where to place this issue, but I thought it might be interesting for all users of the shared database. Otherwise I can of course move it to EU.

I am currently debugging an error the pepquery2 tool. The job errored because the JVM run out of memory.
When I tried to run the job locally I had to stop at 14G because my laptop (16G) started to lag.
I noticed that:
- it uses 8 cores (from the logs) even though it was allocated 1 core
- the index creation set is much slower on the server than on my laptop (might be storage related)
- there is no rule for TPV (and was no rule for sortinghat)

I would like to change that, but I am not sure which values I should consider. In their documentation I found a recommendation for `8 GB of memory and 4 CPUs` which is too little for at least the job I am looking at. When I tried to use `gxadmin query tool-memory-per-inputs` I found:
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429","id":1815495210,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5sNkIq","number":16429,"title":"Static files per host: order matters, favicon.ico and robots.txt not served correctly","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-07-21T09:38:47Z","updated_at":"2023-07-21T09:38:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
1. When specifying static_*_by_host in the configuration, the order of the entries matters, which can lead to Galaxy not serving the specified subdomains correctly.
2. For resources that have no PATH_INFO, like `<galaxy.domain>/robots.txt` and `<galaxy.domain>/favicon.ico` the static configuration by host is not working – the part where the configuration is read will never be reached.  

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**  

Galaxy Version: 23.0
Commit: 2eb6888249fe391bf2c0b8bc563f7ac5bef89d81

**To Reproduce**
1. Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1.1 Serve static configuration with galaxy per host. e.g. add the following to `galaxy.yml`
~~~yaml
    enable_static: true
    static_welcome_html_by_host:
      'example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static/welcome.html/'
      'subdomain1.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain1/welcome.html/'
      'subdomain2.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain2/welcome.html/'
~~~
1.2 Make sure to create the corresponding directories and copy the file structure from `static` to the new static directories.
1.3 Change index.html in `../static-*/welcome.html/index.html` so you can identify them easily.
1.4 Try to load the different subdomains. All subdomains are now being served the `index.html` from `example.org`
1.5 Change the order and move the base domain to the bottom. Everything should work as expected.
2. Steps to reproduce the behavior:
2.1 Serve static configuration with galaxy per host. e.g. add the following to `galaxy.yml`
  ~~~yaml
    enable_static: true
    static_robots_txt_by_host:
      'example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static/robots.txt'
      'subdomain1.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain1/robots.txt'
      'subdomain2.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain2/robots.txt'
  ~~~
2.2 Change the `robots.txt` files in the different directories in a distinguishable way.
2.3 Load the different hosts `example.org/robots.txt` `subdomain1.example.org/robots.txt` and `subdomain1.example.org/robots.txt`. The served file does not show any changes at all, but is the default file.


**Expected behavior**
Galaxy should serve the correct files from the paths configured per host.

**Additional context**
The first issue is probably related to an if statement in [lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py
](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L39)  
Because `example.org in *.example.org` will always evaluate to true.

I think the second error is related to empty `PATH_INFO`. The function does not reach the ["per host configuration"](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L38-L41) part, because it returns, when [this if condition evaluates to true](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L21).
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16429/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

system"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-07-21T09:38:47Z","updated_at":"2023-07-24T15:02:43Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
related to:
- https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/12379
1. When specifying static_*_by_host in the configuration, the order of the entries matters, which can lead to Galaxy not serving the specified subdomains correctly.
2. For resources that have no PATH_INFO, like `<galaxy.domain>/robots.txt` and `<galaxy.domain>/favicon.ico` the static configuration by host is not working – the part where the configuration is read will never be reached.  

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**  

Galaxy Version: 23.0
Commit: 2eb6888249fe391bf2c0b8bc563f7ac5bef89d81

**To Reproduce**
1. Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1.1 Serve static configuration with galaxy per host. e.g. add the following to `galaxy.yml`
~~~yaml
    enable_static: true
    static_welcome_html_by_host:
      'example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static/welcome.html/'
      'subdomain1.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain1/welcome.html/'
      'subdomain2.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain2/welcome.html/'
~~~
1.2 Make sure to create the corresponding directories and copy the file structure from `static` to the new static directories.
1.3 Change index.html in `../static-*/welcome.html/index.html` so you can identify them easily.
1.4 Try to load the different subdomains. All subdomains are now being served the `index.html` from `example.org`
1.5 Change the order and move the base domain to the bottom. Everything should work as expected.
2. Steps to reproduce the behavior:
2.1 Serve static configuration with galaxy per host. e.g. add the following to `galaxy.yml`
  ~~~yaml
    enable_static: true
    static_robots_txt_by_host:
      'example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static/robots.txt'
      'subdomain1.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain1/robots.txt'
      'subdomain2.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain2/robots.txt'
  ~~~
2.2 Change the `robots.txt` files in the different directories in a distinguishable way.
2.3 Load the different hosts `example.org/robots.txt` `subdomain1.example.org/robots.txt` and `subdomain1.example.org/robots.txt`. The served file does not show any changes at all, but is the default file.


**Expected behavior**
Galaxy should serve the correct files from the paths configured per host.

**Additional context**
The first issue is probably related to an if statement in [lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py
](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L39)  
Because `example.org in *.example.org` will always evaluate to true.

I think the second error is related to empty `PATH_INFO`. The function does not reach the ["per host configuration"](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L38-L41) part, because it returns, when [this if condition evaluates to true](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L21).

`lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py`
~~~diff
--- a/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py
+++ b/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py
@@ -18,7 +18,10 @@ class CacheableStaticURLParser(StaticURLParser):
 
     def __call__(self, environ, start_response):
         path_info = environ.get("PATH_INFO", "")
-        if not path_info:
+        script_name = environ.get("SCRIPT_NAME", "")
+        if script_name == "/robots.txt" or script_name == "/favicon.ico":
+            filename = script_name.replace('/', '')
+        elif not path_info:
             # See if this is a static file hackishly mapped.
             if os.path.exists(self.directory) and os.path.isfile(self.directory):
                 app = FileApp(self.directory)
@@ -26,7 +29,7 @@ class CacheableStaticURLParser(StaticURLParser):
                     app.cache_control(max_age=int(self.cache_seconds))
                 return app(environ, start_response)
             return self.add_slash(environ, start_response)
-        if path_info == "/":
+        elif path_info == "/":
             # @@: This should obviously be configurable
             filename = "index.html"
         else:
~~~","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/1648085245/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

the bug**
related to:
- https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/12379
1. When specifying static_*_by_host in the configuration, the order of the entries matters, which can lead to Galaxy not serving the specified subdomains correctly.
2. For resources that have no PATH_INFO, like `<galaxy.domain>/robots.txt` and `<galaxy.domain>/favicon.ico` the static configuration by host is not working – the part where the configuration is read will never be reached.  

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**  

Galaxy Version: 23.0
Commit: 2eb6888249fe391bf2c0b8bc563f7ac5bef89d81

**To Reproduce**
1. Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1.1 Serve static configuration with galaxy per host. e.g. add the following to `galaxy.yml`
~~~yaml
    enable_static: true
    static_welcome_html_by_host:
      'example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static/welcome.html/'
      'subdomain1.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain1/welcome.html/'
      'subdomain2.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain2/welcome.html/'
~~~
1.2 Make sure to create the corresponding directories and copy the file structure from `static` to the new static directories.
1.3 Change index.html in `../static-*/welcome.html/index.html` so you can identify them easily.
1.4 Try to load the different subdomains. All subdomains are now being served the `index.html` from `example.org`
1.5 Change the order and move the base domain to the bottom. Everything should work as expected.
2. Steps to reproduce the behavior:
2.1 Serve static configuration with galaxy per host. e.g. add the following to `galaxy.yml`
  ~~~yaml
    enable_static: true
    static_robots_txt_by_host:
      'example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static/robots.txt'
      'subdomain1.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain1/robots.txt'
      'subdomain2.example.org': '/srv/galaxy/server/static-subdomain2/robots.txt'
  ~~~
2.2 Change the `robots.txt` files in the different directories in a distinguishable way.
2.3 Load the different hosts `example.org/robots.txt` `subdomain1.example.org/robots.txt` and `subdomain1.example.org/robots.txt`. The served file does not show any changes at all, but is the default file.


**Expected behavior**
Galaxy should serve the correct files from the paths configured per host.

**Additional context**
The first issue is probably related to an if statement in [lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py
](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L39)  
Because `example.org in *.example.org` will always evaluate to true.

I think the second error is related to empty `PATH_INFO`. The function does not reach the ["per host configuration"](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L38-L41) part, because it returns, when [this if condition evaluates to true](https://github.com/jmchilton/galaxy/blob/43b6eb2e1f200eb25d710995af4fdb8e762314a5/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/static.py#L21).

-<!--div class="alert" style="background: #FFD500;">
+<div class="alert" style="background: #f8b63e;">
 
-The Freiburg Galaxy team will be striking on 03.03.2023.
-For more information, please see our [blog post](https://galaxyproject.org/news/2023-02-27-climate-strike/).
+#### **Maintenance: Dynamic job router switch**
 
-</div-->
+The European Galaxy server will switch to a new job router, [Total Perspective Vortex](https://total-perspective-vortex.readthedocs.io/).
+It helps us to keep resource management simple and in-sync with other usegalaxy.* instances.
+We have thoroughly tested our new setup. However, we cannot exclude the possibility that there may be temporary disruptions in job routing and that some jobs may not start. We will try to keep this time as short as possible and expect that these restrictions will be over by tomorrow. We apologise for any inconvenience this may -<!--div class="alert" style="background: #FFD500;">
+<div class="alert" style="background: #f8b63e;">
 
-The Freiburg Galaxy team will be striking on 03.03.2023.
-For more information, please see our [blog post](https://galaxyproject.org/news/2023-02-27-climate-strike/).
+#### **Maintenance: Dynamic job router switch**
 
-</div-->
+The European Galaxy server will switch to a new job router, [Total Perspective Vortex](https://total-perspective-vortex.readthedocs.io/).
+It helps us to keep resource management simple and in-sync with other usegalaxy.* instances.
+We have thoroughly tested our new setup. However, we cannot exclude the possibility that there may be temporary disruptions in job routing and that some jobs may not start. We will try to keep this time as short as possible and expect that these restrictions will be over by tomorrow. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.
+
+The maintenance period starts today, May 3rd from 4 pm CEST and should last until tomorrow May 4th.","path":"content/bare/eu/usegalaxy/notices.md","commit_id":"0c073ccbf4262eace85178e4f246b0f04608b9fb","original_commit_id":"0c073ccbf4262eace85178e4f246b0f04608b9fb","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

-The Freiburg Galaxy team will be striking on 03.03.2023.
-For more information, please see our [blog post](https://galaxyproject.org/news/2023-02-27-climate-strike/).
+#### **Maintenance: Dynamic job router switch**
 
-</div-->
+The European Galaxy server will switch to a new job router, [Total Perspective Vortex](https://total-perspective-vortex.readthedocs.io/).
+It helps us to keep resource management simple and in-sync with other usegalaxy.* instances.
+We have thoroughly tested our new setup. However, we cannot exclude the possibility that there may be temporary disruptions in job routing and that some jobs may not start. We will try to keep this time as short as possible and expect that these restrictions will be over by tomorrow. +# Mothballing the Sorting Hat
+
+## An old hat
+usegalaxy.eu runs more than 1 mio jobs per month. All these jobs have different demands and use different resources and technologies, like Docker, Apptainer etc., which need all sorts of parameters and environment variables. Galaxy handles jobs with resource managers like SLURM, HTCondor or the remote job execution system Pulsar.
+But how do these resource managers know which resources each individual job needs?  
+You could now specify that for every single tool, but as you can guess, with over 8.000 tools and many different possibilities to compute a job, this approach leads to chaotic unmaintainable static ssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/15131","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15131","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15131.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15131.patch","merged_at":"2023-01-20T03:00:28Z"},"body":"This PR aims to support a simple way of supplying genetic variants to the beacon network - directly from the galaxy UI.

## Whats the beacon network
The [beacon network](https://beacon-network.org/) is a search engine for genetic variants developed by the [Global Alliance for Genomics and Health ](https://www.ga4gh.org/).
Variants are provided by a network of individual web services called [beacons](https://beacon-network.org/#/about).

These beacons can be queried for _specific variants_ (chromosome name, start position, reference genome name, reference sequence, alternate sequence) which have to be known to the query issuer. Upon such a query they will respond with "Yes" if the beacon knows of such a variant and "No" otherwise

## Why supply data from galaxy
Making variant data available over a beacon creates opportunities to be found and referenced by other researchers. Most importantly this is achieved **without publishing the actual data**, which removes a major hurdle for some data.

However, requiring researchers to maintain a web service and supply data directly to its database will stop most from participating. Hence the idea to make it available to all galaxy.eu users, directly from the platform they are already using

## What is changed

The actual beacon integration will be handled by a galaxy admin running a [python script](https://github.com/Paprikant/galaxy-beacon-import) which will build a beacon database from datasets shared by galaxy users.
The impact on galaxy is minimal by design. However, galaxy is getting some changes to provide some information and safeguard the datasets.

* A new user preference `beacon_enabled` is added. Data of a user will only ever be accessed after the user has manually enabled this preference (and therefore hopefully read the the associated texts)
* A vue component is added to the user settings, displaying a modal with further information and buttons to change beacon settings and switch to the associated histories
* A new admin preference is added, which enables or disables beacon sharing system wide

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15705080/206169595-ef58a8b3-3d71-4b4a-9d0e-039539cca57e.png)

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

encountered a few problems yesterday, when I helped [@engynasr](https://github.com/engynasr) to upload her datasets in a Data Library.

1.  The folder upload is only possible for admin users, and only by rsync-ing the files to our galaxy's server
2.  After the upload, I was able to import the files with the admin-only path-import-tool, but it showed an red error pop-up and I dont know if everything worked correctly.
3.  The permissions are automatically set to my user only for all the datasets and I don't see a way to change this efficiently (we have 942 Files, every single one would have to be changed manually

My suggestions:
 - implement a folder upload for users in the Data Libraries. This could also be tied to a certain role or manual permission from admins to prevent abuse
 - make it possible to mutate permissions for all datasets in folders and sub-folders at once
 - fix popups or show errors more accurately and remove '200% completed' text in upload window.
 - if we keep the admin path upload, please specify which paths are allowed, because not all locations worked for me
 
Thank you in advance. I think this could make life easier for both admins and users :)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/1495458797","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007#issuecomment-1495458797","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007","id":1495458797,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAdw-Nc5ZIuPt","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-04-04T07:09:22Z","updated_at":"2023-04-04T07:09:22Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Thank you :) I created this ephemeris command: https://github.com/galaxyproject/ephemeris/pull/187

Yes it is a different installation process. https://github.com/CyVerse-Ansible/ansible-singularity/blob/master/tasks/singularity-post-3.yml
For RHEL systems, you can simply install it with `dnf install apptainer`
But I think it is different for Ubuntu
according to my google research:
```
sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:apptainer/ppa
sudo apt install -y apptainer
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/training-material/issues/comments/1495638685/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

observe mixed behaviour in my Pulsar endpoint:
 - everything works, the job is green, files are transferred, everything fine
 - the job is green, files are not transferred, but exist in the output dir
 - job is red, files are not transferred, but visible in the pulsar staging dir `working/out`
 - job is red, no files are produced, but when the command is run manually, it works and produces output files
 - job is green, no files are produced, but when the command is run manually, it works and produces output files

Currently I am unable to spot any pattern here or any reliable setting that works. I will try to observe changing parts around it more.
What adds up to it might be that some tools are not working as expected, e.g. ENAsearch is producing wrong output for xml.
And Galaxy Handlers are not picking up status messages sometimes, which is fixed by a handler restart

#### OS
RockyLinux 9.1
#### Pulsar
pulsar-app==0.14.13
#### Galaxy

observe mixed behaviour in my Pulsar endpoint:
 - everything works, the job is green, files are transferred, everything fine
 - the job is green, files are not transferred, but exist in the output dir
 - job is red, files are not transferred, but visible in the pulsar staging dir `working/out`
 - job is red, no files are produced, but when the command is run manually, it works and produces output files
 - job is green, no files are produced, but when the command is run manually, it works and produces output files

Currently I am unable to spot any pattern here or any reliable setting that works. I will try to observe changing parts around it more.
What adds up to it might be that some tools are not working as expected, e.g. ENAsearch is producing wrong output for xml.
And Galaxy Handlers are not picking up status messages sometimes, which is fixed by a handler restart

#### OS
RockyLinux 9.1
#### Pulsar
pulsar-app==0.14.13
#### Galaxy

rs/mvdbeek/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"assignees":[{"login":"mvdbeek","id":6804901,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY4MDQ5MDE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6804901?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek","html_url":"https://github.com/mvdbeek","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mvdbeek/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2022-11-05T15:33:23Z","updated_at":"2022-12-02T11:17:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We have been getting a lot of error reports like:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/galaxy/server/lib/galaxy/jobs/runners/__init__.py", line 628, in _finish_or_resubmit_job
    job_wrapper.finish(
  File "/opt/galaxy/server/lib/galaxy/jobs/__init__.py", line 1802, in finish
    import_model_store = store.get_import_model_store_for_directory(
  File "/opt/galaxy/server/lib/galaxy/model/store/__init__.py", line 1222, in get_import_model_store_for_directory
    raise Exception(
Exception: Could not find import model store for directory [/data/jwd/main/051/948/51948333/metadata/outputs_populated] (full path [/data/jwd/main/051/948/51948333/metadata/outputs_populated])
```

This seems to be true, but it was not obvious why this has happen

```
galaxy@sn06:~$ ll /data/jwd/main/051/948/51948333/metadata/
total 148
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy     20 Nov  5 08:53 metadata_kwds_tab
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy      0 Nov  5 08:53 metadata_out_tab
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy      2 Nov  5 08:53 metadata_override_tab
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy     41 Nov  5 08:53 metadata_results_tab
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy  10775 Nov  5 08:53 object_store_conf.json
drwxr-xr-x 2 galaxy galaxy   4096 Nov  5 08:53 outputs_new
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy   1910 Nov  5 08:53 params.json
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy 120181 Nov  5 08:53 registry.xml
-rw-r--r-- 1 galaxy galaxy     73 Nov  5 08:53 set.py
```

At first, that seems like a problem with `metadata_strategy: 'extended'` which we have enabled by default. Disabling this revealed a problem with setting metadata in general, which is handled by celery in our setup.

It turned out that a recent Galaxy update has introduced a new dependency that was not synced to the external celery node. So celery was the problem behind this error. Fixing celery and refreshing the venv of celery fixed it.

Not sure if we can improve the initial error message, to direct admins directly to the set_metadata process.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19","id":1434764866,"node_id":"I_kwDODjMX9c5VhMZC","number":19,"title":"Socket creation","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-11-03T14:33:28Z","updated_at":"2022-11-03T14:33:28Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We tried the new version (2.1.3) but encountered a problem with the socket creation:
[hardcoded](https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/blob/577d68992ece63f208602c00ec3a10e86ffe746d/tasks/main.yml#L99) in the role, the sock will be created in `/etc/systemd/system/tiaas.socket`, but by default, the role references with `tiaas_socket: /run/tiaas.sock`.  
We solved the problem by creating a symlink `ln -s /run/tiaas.sock /etc/systemd/system/tiaas.sock`  
Without the symlink, it failed due to missing permission (systemd/system is root) and the tiaas2 user has no access.

Obviously, a quick fix would be to hardcode this symlink creation, but I don't know if this is a clean solution.

A smaller, second error was, that we still had `tiaas_info.domain: "https://usegalaxy.eu"` instead of "usegalaxy.eu", so the ALLOWED_HOSTS failed.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

[hardcoded](https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/blob/577d68992ece63f208602c00ec3a10e86ffe746d/tasks/main.yml#L99) in the role, the sock will be created in `/etc/systemd/system/tiaas.socket`, but by default, the role references with `tiaas_socket: /run/tiaas.sock`.  
We solved the problem by creating a symlink `ln -s /run/tiaas.sock /etc/systemd/system/tiaas.socket`  
Without the symlink, it failed due to missing permission (systemd/system is root) and the tiaas2 user has no access.

Obviously, a quick fix would be to hardcode this symlink creation, but I don't know if this is a clean solution.

A smaller, second error was, that we still had `tiaas_info.domain: "https://usegalaxy.eu"` instead of "usegalaxy.eu", so the ALLOWED_HOSTS failed.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/comments/1303178437","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19#issuecomment-1303178437","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19","id":1303178437,"node_id":"IC_kwDODjMX9c5NrOzF","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-11-04T09:39:10Z","updated_at":"2022-11-04T09:39:10Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Update:

had the idea to make a small CLI TPV tool, that takes your config files, a few input params like
```gnome-terminal
$ tpv-dry -d /.../config/tpv tool=metaspades user=test_user@galaxyproject.eu size=2G
```
and then mocks a Galaxy instance, like in the unit tests, calculates the destination and gives a easy-to-read output, which destination was chosen and why. eg:

```gnome-terminal
RESULT for .....
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESTINATION
final: condor_tpv
mached(with ranks): condor_tpv(1), singularity(2), interactive(3)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARAMS
cores: 8 (min of tool: 10, user: 8, role: 9)
mem: ....
...
```
Additionally an explanation of how the rank was calculated in this specific case, would be helpful to debug errors in the configuration and check if it works as intentioned.
EU will work on this, but all help and suggestions are appreciated :)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/48/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/48/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
