We solved the problem by creating a symlink `ln -s /run/tiaas.sock /etc/systemd/system/tiaas.socket`  
Without the symlink, it failed due to missing permission (systemd/system is root) and the tiaas2 user has no access.

Obviously, a quick fix would be to hardcode this symlink creation, but I don't know if this is a clean solution.

A smaller, second error was, that we still had `tiaas_info.domain: "https://usegalaxy.eu"` instead of "usegalaxy.eu", so the ALLOWED_HOSTS failed.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-tiaas2/issues/19/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"reopened"}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007","id":1453170987,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5WnaEr","number":15007,"title":"More convienient ways to create Data Libraries","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-11-17T11:20:36Z","updated_at":"2022-11-17T11:20:36Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I encountered a few problems yesterday, when I helped [@engynasr](https://github.com/engynasr) to upload her datasets in a Data Library.

1.  The folder upload is only possible for admin users, and only by rsync-ing the files to our galaxy's server
2.  After the upload, I was able to import the files with the admin-only path-import-tool, but it showed an red error pop-up and I dont know if everything worked correctly.
3.  The permissions are automatically set to my user only for all the datasets and I don't see a way to change this efficiently (we have 942 Files, every single one would have to be changed manually

My suggestions:
 - implement a folder upload for users in the Data Libraries. This could also be tied to a certain role or manual permission from admins to prevent abuse
 - make it possible to mutate permissions for all datasets in folders and sub-folders at once
 - fix popups or show errors more accurately and remove '200% completed' text in upload window.
 - if we keep the admin path upload, please specify which paths are allowed, because not all locations worked for me
 
Thank you in advance. I think this could make life easier for both admins and users :)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

on discussion in #113 to a newer specification. Now all of the following are supported:
 
```
<required>
<required|or|some|ignored|things> # Useful for <user|id|email> which is stored as arg_user
[optional]
[optional=default]
[--flag]  # Proper flag, boolean yes/undefined.
[--flag=] # No default but it's not a flag
[--flag=default] # Some default
```

This replaces the previous version of wap with some additional updates, notably these are now identical

```
--limit=123
--limit 123
```

had the idea to make a small CLI TPV tool, that takes your config files, a few input params like
```gnome-terminal
$ tpv-dry -d /.../config/tpv tool=metaspades user=test_user@galaxyproject.eu size=2G
```
and then mocks a Galaxy instance, like in the unit tests, calculates the destination and gives a easy-to-read output, which destination was chosen and why. eg:

```gnome-terminal
RESULT for .....
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESTINATION
final: condor_tpv
mached(with ranks): condor_tpv(1), singularity(2), interactive(3)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARAMS
cores: 8 (min of tool: 10, user: 8, role: 9)
mem: ....
...
```
Additionally an explanation of how the rank was calculated in this specific case, would be helpful to debug errors in the configuration and check if it works as intentioned.
EU will work on this, but all help and suggestions are appreciated ```

added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. +#!/usr/bin/env python
+'''Tool to set permissions for all datasets of a given Galaxy Data Library'''
+
+import argparse
+import logging as log
+from bioblend import galaxy
+import sys, time, os
+
+from .common_parser import get_common_args
+# Print iterations progress
+def printProgressBar (iteration, total, prefix = '', suffix = '', decimals = 1, length = 100, fill = '█', printEnd = "\r"):
+    """
+    Call in a loop to create terminal progress bar
+    @params:
+        iteration   - Required  : current iteration (Int)
+        total       - Required  : total iterations (Int)
+        prefix      - Optional  : prefix string (Str)
+        suffix      - Optional  : suffix string (Str)
+        decimals    - Optional  : positive number of decimals in percent complete (Int)
+        length      - Optional  : character length of bar (Int)
+        fill        - Optional  : bar fill character (Str)
+        printEnd    - Optional  : end character (e.g. "\r", "\r\n") (Str)
+    """
+    percent = ("{0:." + str(decimals) + "f}").format(100 * (iteration / float(total)))
+    filledLength = int(length * iteration // total)
+    bar = fill * filledLength + '-' * (length - filledLength)
+    print(f'\r{prefix} |{bar}| {percent}% {suffix}', end = printEnd)
+    # Print New Line on Complete
+    if iteration == total: 
+        print()
+
+def get_datasets(gi, library_id) -> [str]:
+    objects = gi.libraries.show_dataset(library_id=library_id, dataset_id='')
+    datasets = []
+    for index in range(len(objects)):
+       if objects[index]['type'] == 'file':
+           datasets.append(objects[index]['id'])
+    if datasets == []:
+        sys.exit("No datasets in library!")
+    else:
+        return datasets
+
+def set_permissions(gi, library_id, role_ids):
+    log.info("Your library_id is " + library_id + "\n")
+    role_msg = "Your roles are: " + " ".join(role_ids)
+    log.info(role_msg)
+    datasets = get_datasets(gi, library_id)
+    total = len(datasets)
+    est = total*3/60
+    # Give User time to abort
+    log.info('\nSuccess! %d datasets found. Processing can take up to %f min', total, est)
+    t = 5
+    while t:
+        log.info('Starting in %d s ... Press Crtl+C to abort.', t)
+        time.sleep(1)

added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. I don't have experience with this so please tell me if I should change added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. I don't have experience with this so please tell me if I should change added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. I don't have experience with this so please tell me if I should change added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. I don't have experience with this so please tell me if I should change +#!/usr/bin/env python
+'''Tool to set permissions for all datasets of a given Galaxy Data Library'''
+
+import argparse
+import logging as log
+from bioblend import galaxy
+import sys, time, os
+
+from .common_parser import get_common_args
+# Print iterations progress
+def printProgressBar (iteration, total, prefix = '', suffix = '', decimals = 1, length = 100, fill = '█', printEnd = "\r"):
+    """
+    Call in a loop to create terminal progress bar
+    @params:
+        iteration   - Required  : current iteration (Int)
+        total       - Required  : total iterations (Int)
+        prefix      - Optional  : prefix string (Str)
+        suffix      - Optional  : suffix string (Str)
+        decimals    - Optional  : positive number of decimals in percent complete (Int)
+        length      - Optional  : character length of bar (Int)
+        fill        - Optional  : bar fill character (Str)
+        printEnd    - Optional  : end character (e.g. "\r", "\r\n") (Str)
+    """
+    percent = ("{0:." + str(decimals) + "f}").format(100 * (iteration / float(total)))
+    filledLength = int(length * iteration // total)
+    bar = fill * filledLength + '-' * (length - filledLength)
+    print(f'\r{prefix} |{bar}| {percent}% {suffix}', end = printEnd)
+    # Print New Line on Complete
+    if iteration == total: 
+        print()
+
+def get_datasets(gi, library_id) -> [str]:
+    objects = gi.libraries.show_dataset(library_id=library_id, dataset_id='')
+    datasets = []
+    for index in range(len(objects)):
+       if objects[index]['type'] == 'file':
+           datasets.append(objects[index]['id'])
+    if datasets == []:
+        sys.exit("No datasets in library!")
+    else:
+        return datasets
+
+def set_permissions(gi, library_id, role_ids):
+    log.info("Your library_id is " + library_id + "\n")
+    role_msg = "Your roles are: " + " ".join(role_ids)
+    log.info(role_msg)
+    datasets = get_datasets(gi, library_id)
+    total = len(datasets)
+    est = total*3/60
+    # Give User time to abort
+    log.info('\nSuccess! %d datasets found. Processing can take up to %f min', total, est)
+    t = 5
+    while t:
+        log.info('Starting in %d s ... Press Crtl+C to abort.', t)

```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. I don't have experience with this so please tell me if I should change +#!/usr/bin/env python
+'''Tool to set permissions for all datasets of a given Galaxy Data Library'''
+
+import argparse
+import logging as log
+from bioblend import galaxy
+import sys, time, os
+from rich.progress import Progress
+from .common_parser import get_common_args
+# Print iterations progress
+
+
+def get_datasets(gi, library_id) -> [str]:
+    objects = gi.libraries.show_dataset(library_id=library_id, dataset_id='')
+    datasets = []
+    for index in range(len(objects)):
+       if objects[index]['type'] == 'file':
+           datasets.append(objects[index]['id'])
+    if datasets == []:
+        sys.exit("No datasets in library!")
+    else:
+        return datasets
+
+def set_permissions(gi, library_id, role_ids):
+    log.info("Your library_id is " + library_id + "\n")
+    log.info("Your roles are: %s", " ".join(role_ids))
+    datasets = get_datasets(gi, library_id)
+    total = len(datasets)
+    est = total*3/60
+    # Give User time to abort
+    log.info('\nSuccess! %d datasets found. Processing can take up to %f min', total, est)
+    if input("Do you want to continue? It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. +#!/usr/bin/env python
+'''Tool to set permissions for all datasets of a given Galaxy Data Library'''
+
+import argparse
+import logging as log
+from bioblend import galaxy
+import sys, time, os
+from rich.progress import Progress
+from .common_parser import get_common_args
+# Print iterations progress
+
+
+def get_datasets(gi, library_id) -> [str]:
+    objects = gi.libraries.show_dataset(library_id=library_id, dataset_id='')
+    datasets = []
+    for index in range(len(objects)):
+       if objects[index]['type'] == 'file':
+           datasets.append(objects[index]['id'])
+    if datasets == []:
+        sys.exit("No datasets in library!")
+    else:
+        return datasets
+
+def set_permissions(gi, library_id, role_ids):
+    log.info("Your library_id is " + library_id + "\n")
+    log.info("Your roles are: %s", " ".join(role_ids))
+    datasets = get_datasets(gi, library_id)
+    total = len(datasets)
+    est = total*3/60
+    # Give User time to abort
+    log.info('\nSuccess! %d datasets found. Processing can take up to %f min', total, est)
+    if input("Do you want to continue? (y/n) ") == "y":

added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. added a tool to set the permissions of ALL datasets in a given data library (in folders and subfolders) at once to the given roles.
It is a workaround for one point of this [Issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15007), I created.

Usage:
```
usage: set_library_permissions [-h] [-v] [-g GALAXY] [-u USER] [-p PASSWORD] [-a API_KEY] [--library LIBRARY] [--roles ROLES]
```

Currently the default loglevel is INFO. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74","id":1391363637,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdVyGs5S7oY1","number":74,"title":"Zero downtime restarts","user":{"login":"natefoo","id":286969,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk2OQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/286969?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo","html_url":"https://github.com/natefoo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/natefoo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2022-09-29T19:09:42Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T07:57:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Currently we're relying on [unicornherder](https://github.com/alphagov/unicornherder/) to perform zero-downtime restarts, but this has a few drawbacks. We have patches for some of the biggest issues we're running in to (alphagov/unicornherder#38, alphagov/unicornherder#39) and others that we don't (galaxyproject/galaxy#14625) but as unicornherder seems to not be seeing much activity these days, we would like to consider alternatives.

One option is to try to support `SIGUSR2` restarts with gunicorn directly in Gravity, but you still need some kind of background process management since as far as I know, you cannot keep gunicorn directly attached in the foreground to systemd/supervisord after the `SIGUSR2` restart cycle completes, as discssed in benoitc/gunicorn#2856.

However, in discussion with @hexylena, it seems like we can use the solution that EU was using with uWSGI. On org, I was using [uWSGI Zerg Mode](https://uwsgi-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Zerg.html) to run one zergling at a time, bringing up a second whenever a restart was needed. Bringing up the second signaled the first to shut down, and then things continued normally from there. So at any given time you had one or the other zergling running, but not both.

On EU, they run multiple zerglings run at all times. When a restart is needed, one zergling is restarted, and once that zergling has fully reloaded and is accepting requests, the next zergling is restarted, and so on.

In the case of Gravity, I believe we should be able to have systemd open the socket, and then run two gunicorn workers that use the same fd-over-socket method used by Zerg Mode and [circusd](https://circus.readthedocs.io/en/latest/for-ops/sockets/) (which was actually the initial way we ran gunicorn in development prior to 22.01...). Gravity then will just need to manage the restart dance.

The only thing I don't like about this scenario is that under supervisord, you have two logfiles, one for each worker, which is kind of a bummer. But this is mitigated in a systemd setup where everything logs to journald, so you can just request the combined logs using a journalctl unit matching pattern.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/comments/1281966068","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74#issuecomment-1281966068","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/gravity/issues/74","id":1281966068,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAdVyGs5MaT_0","user":{"login":"mira-miracoli","id":86979912,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2OTc5OTEy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86979912?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli","html_url":"https://github.com/mira-miracoli","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mira-miracoli/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-10-18T07:57:14Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T07:57:14Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"We are currently thinking about using two headnodes, so we have no downtime during OS upgrades etc.
